title: The 9 Best Herbal Tinctures for Permanently Stopping Eye Twitching

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid is good for eye twitching because it has anti-inflammatory and anti-anxiety properties that can help to reduce muscle spasms and promote relaxation, thereby alleviating the twitching sensation in the eyes.
Valeranone is good for eye twitching because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the muscles around the eyes, reducing spasms and twitching.
Valerenal is good for eye twitching because it has a calming effect on the nerves, reducing muscle spasms and relieving irritation that can cause eye twitching.
Isovaleric acid is good for eye twitching because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce muscle spasms and calm the nerves around the eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, is a natural herb that can help with eye twitching. The tincture made from this herb is a liquid extract that contains the plant's active ingredients. Eye twitching, or blepharospasm, can be caused by stress, fatigue, or other factors. Valerian root tincture has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce muscle spasms and twitching in the eyes. It contains compounds like valerenic acid and valepotriates, which have a sedative effect on the body. When taken as directed, valerian root tincture can help to relax the muscles and calm the nervous system, potentially reducing the frequency and severity of eye twitching. This can be especially helpful for people who experience eye twitching due to stress or anxiety. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms, valerian root tincture may provide relief from eye twitching and promote overall eye health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures that help with eye twitching, start by gathering high-quality dried roots of the licorice plant, also known as Glycyrrhiza glabra. Clean the roots thoroughly and chop them into small pieces. Combine 1 part of the chopped roots with 2 parts of 80% to 100% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar. Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar gently every day. This process allows the roots to release their active compounds into the liquid. After the desired steeping time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container. Discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool, dark place. To use, take 20-30 drops of the tincture in water or another liquid as needed to help alleviate eye twitching. Note that you should consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or take medications.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal tinctures for eye twitching

Kaempferol is good for eye twitching because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the muscles around the eyes, which can cause twitching.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata tincture is a natural remedy that may help with eye twitching. This tincture is made from the passionflower plant, a herb that has been used for centuries to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety. Eye twitching, also known as blepharospasm, can be caused by stress, fatigue, or irritation. The tincture works by influencing the nerve pathways in the brain, which can help to calm the muscles in the face and eyes. When the muscles relax, eye twitching can stop. The passionflower plant contains chemicals that have a sedative effect, which can help to slow down the activity of the nerves and promote relaxation. Some people find that taking Passiflora incarnata tincture helps to reduce their eye twitching and provides relief from the discomfort and annoyance of this condition.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tincture that helps with eye twitching, you will need dried licorice root, a clean glass jar with a lid, vodka or another high-proof alcohol, and a dark glass bottle for storing the tincture. First, measure out one part dried licorice root and combine it with two parts vodka in the glass jar. Make sure the licorice root is fully covered by the vodka. Next, seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the licorice root extract into the vodka. After the two to three weeks, strain the liquid from the jar through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle. Discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture, place a few drops under your tongue or mix it with water and drink it. The licorice root in the tincture is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe and calm the muscles around your eyes, reducing eye twitching.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tincture is a natural remedy that can help with eye twitching. Eye twitching, or blepharospasm, is a common condition where the eyelid rapidly moves up and down. This can be caused by stress, fatigue, or even too much caffeine. Avena sativa, also known as oats, is a plant that has been used for centuries to calm the nervous system. The tincture is made from the seeds of the oats plant and is often used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness. When taken, it can help to calm the nervous system, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote relaxation. This can, in turn, help to reduce eye twitching by addressing the underlying causes. Avena sativa tincture is also thought to have a direct effect on the nervous system, helping to relax the muscles and reduce spasms. Many people find that taking Avena sativa tincture helps to alleviate eye twitching and other symptoms associated with nervous tension.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture for eye twitching, start by gathering the required ingredients: dried licorice root, a clean glass jar, a glass measuring cup, and a neutral-tasting oil like sunflower or grapeseed oil. Fill a glass jar with 1 part of dried licorice root and 2 parts of the neutral-tasting oil, making sure the licorice root is completely covered by the oil. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the ingredients mix. After the maceration period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Transfer the liquid tincture to a dropper bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. When using the tincture, place 10-20 drops under the tongue or mix with water, 3-4 times a day, as needed. Note that it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are taking medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis tincture is a natural remedy that can help with eye twitching. This tincture is made from the extract of the lemon balm plant, which has been used for centuries to calm the nervous system. Eye twitching is often caused by stress, anxiety, or fatigue, and Melissa officinalis has a soothing effect on the body. The tannins and flavonoids present in the plant help to relax the muscles and calm the nervous system, which can lead to a reduction in eye twitching. Additionally, Melissa officinalis has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tincture that helps with eye twitching, start by gathering dried licorice root, a digital thermometer, a glass jar with a lid, and a high-proof solvent like vodka or glycerin. Combine one part of dried licorice root with two parts of the solvent in the glass jar, making sure the root is completely covered. Secure the lid tightly and store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar well every day. This process allows the solvent to extract the active compounds from the licorice root. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Transfer the tincture to a smaller glass bottle and label it. The final step is to use a dropper to take 20-30 drops of the tincture, 2-3 times a day, mixed with water or another liquid, to help alleviate eye twitching.

The calming compounds in English lavender help to relax the muscles, reducing the twitching. The tincture of English lavender is made by soaking the plant's flowers in a liquid, which captures the plant's active ingredients. These ingredients are then absorbed into the body when the tincture is taken, helping to calm the muscles and reduce eye twitching. This is why some people find English lavender tincture helpful in reducing eye twitching.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tincture is a natural remedy that may help with eye twitching. Eye twitching, also known as blepharospasm, is a common condition where the eyelid muscles contract and cause twitching. Ginkgo biloba is an ancient plant that has been used for centuries to improve blood flow to the brain and other parts of the body. When taken as a tincture, it may help relax the eyelid muscles and increase blood flow to the eyes. This can help reduce the frequency and severity of eye twitching. Ginkgo biloba tincture may also have antioxidant properties that can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, it may help improve circulation and reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye twitching. Some people find that taking Ginkgo biloba tincture helps them relax and reduces stress, which can also contribute to eye twitching.

Make sure the jar is sterilized and the materials are handled in a clean environment. Add the dried root to the solvent and seal the jar tightly. remedies.remedy_desc: Scutellaria baicalensis tincture is a herbal remedy that may help with eye twitching. This plant, also known as Baikal skullcap, has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries. It contains compounds like baicalein and baicalin, which have anti-inflammatory and calming properties. When applied topically or taken orally as a tincture, these compounds may help reduce eye irritation and spasms that cause twitching. Eye twitching can be caused by stress, fatigue, or exposure to chemicals, and Scutellaria baicalensis tincture may help alleviate these underlying issues. Some people also find that it helps relax the eye muscles, which can become tense and cause twitching. However, it's essential to note that results may vary, and individual experiences may differ. More research is needed to fully understand the effects of Scutellaria baicalensis tincture on eye twitching.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture for eye twitching, you will need dried roots of the licorice plant, vodka or a similar high-proof spirit, and a clean glass jar. First, crush the dried roots into small pieces to release their active compounds. Fill a clean glass jar about one-quarter full with the crushed roots. Then, pour vodka over the roots until they are completely covered, making sure the liquid is about an inch above the roots. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the roots release their medicine into the liquid. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting liquid is your Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture. To use, take 20-30 drops of the tincture in water, 2-3 times a day, as needed to help alleviate eye twitching. It's essential to note that tinctures should be made in a well-ventilated area and handled with caution, as some people may experience allergic reactions to certain herbs.

intro_desc: If you're experiencing eye twitching, it's essential to address the issue as it can be a sign of an underlying problem, such as stress, fatigue, or even a nutrient deficiency. When left unchecked, eye twitching can lead to eye strain, headaches, and difficulty sleeping. To alleviate eye twitching, consider using herbal tinctures. Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, is a natural relaxant that can help calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. It contains a compound called valerenic acid, which has a soothing effect on the muscles, including the ones around the eyes.  Another herb that can be beneficial for eye twitching is Passiflora incarnata, or passionflower. This herb has been used for centuries to treat anxiety and insomnia, both of which can contribute to eye twitching. Passionflower contains flavonoids and alkaloids that help reduce anxiety and promote a restful sleep. When combined with valerian root, these two herbs can provide a powerful combination to calm the nervous system and alleviate eye twitching.  To fix the issue of eye twitching, it's crucial to identify and address the underlying cause. This can be achieved through a combination of herbal remedies, relaxation techniques, and lifestyle changes. For example, practicing stress-reducing activities like meditation or yoga, getting regular exercise, and maintaining a balanced diet can help alleviate eye twitching. By addressing the root cause of the issue, you can improve your overall well-being and quality of life.
supplementary_best: To remedy eye twitching, a combination of herbal tinctures can be effective. A blend of Passiflora incarnata and Valeriana officinalis is a good starting point, as these herbs are known to calm the nervous system and reduce muscle spasms. Passiflora incarnata, also known as passionflower, contains compounds that help regulate the nervous system and reduce anxiety, which can contribute to eye twitching. Valeriana officinalis, or valerian root, is a natural sedative that can help relax the muscles and reduce spasms.  Avena sativa, or oat straw, can be added to this combination to provide additional benefits. Avena sativa is known to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation, which can help reduce eye twitching. It can also help to rebalance the body's natural energy and reduce stress.  Melissa officinalis, or lemon balm, can be added to the blend to provide a soothing and calming effect. It contains compounds that can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making it an effective addition to this combination.  The combination of Passiflora incarnata, Valeriana officinalis, Avena sativa, and Melissa officinalis provides a balanced approach to addressing eye twitching. These herbs work together to calm the nervous system, reduce muscle spasms, and promote relaxation, making them a well-rounded and effective remedy for eye twitching.  Note: Before using any herbal remedies, it is recommended to consult with a healthcare professional to ensure safe use and to rule out any underlying medical conditions that may be contributing to eye twitching.
supplementary_avoid: If you have eye twitching, it's best to avoid herbal tinctures that may worsen the condition. One such herb is Yohimbe (Pausinystalia johimbe), which can cause eye strain and dryness. This is because Yohimbe can stimulate the nervous system and increase eye pressure, making eye twitching worse. Another herb to avoid is Ephedra (Ephedra sinica), as it can cause eye strain and dryness due to its stimulatory effects on the nervous system. Additionally, Black cohosh (Actaea racemosa) should be avoided as it can cause eye irritation and inflammation. This is because Black cohosh can cause an allergic reaction in some people, leading to eye problems. Lastly, Kola nut (Cola nitida) should be avoided as it can cause eye strain and dryness due to its caffeine content, which can stimulate the nervous system and increase eye pressure.
supplementary_other_remedies: Eye twitching, also known as blepharospasm, can be caused by stress, fatigue, caffeine, or certain medical conditions. Besides herbal tinctures, other natural remedies can help alleviate this issue. One such remedy is warm compresses. Applying a warm compress to the eyes can increase blood flow and relax the muscles, providing instant relief from twitching. This is because the heat from the compress helps to loosen up tense muscles, much like how a warm bath can relax your body after a long day. Another remedy is getting enough sleep. When we're tired, our muscles can become strained and tense, leading to twitching. Getting adequate sleep helps to recharge our bodies, including our eye muscles, and can reduce the frequency of twitching. Some people also find that taking a break from caffeine and electronics, especially before bedtime, can help alleviate eye twitching. This is because both caffeine and electronic screens can stimulate our nervous system, leading to muscle tension and twitching.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience eye twitching may also have other health issues that can be helped with medicinal herbs. For example, stress and anxiety, which can cause eye twitching, can also lead to insomnia. Valerian root and chamomile tea can help with insomnia because they have a calming effect on the body and promote relaxation.  Eye twitching can also be a sign of an underlying nutrient deficiency, such as magnesium or potassium. Ashwagandha and dandelion root can help increase magnesium levels in the body and also have anti-inflammatory properties.  Another common issue that people with eye twitching may experience is dry eyes. Eyebright herb can help with dry eyes by reducing inflammation and increasing tear production. It can also help with allergies that can cause eye twitching.  Some people with eye twitching may also experience headaches, which can be caused by dehydration or stress. Feverfew and ginger can help with headaches by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the brain.  Eye twitching can also be a sign of an overactive thyroid gland (hyperthyroidism). Medicinal herbs like bladderwrack and kelp can help regulate thyroid function and reduce eye twitching.  It's essential to note that while medicinal herbs can help alleviate symptoms, they should not replace medical treatment. It's always best to consult with a healthcare professional before trying any new remedies.
